---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Iyenoma Goodnews Osabuohien"
output: html_document

date: "2026-04-08"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
pacman::p_load(flexable, gt, reactable, tidyverse,
  inspectdf,
  plotly,)
```

#making headings

# R Markdown

## R Markdown

### R Markdown

creating bullets

-   bullet 1
-   bullet 2

#adding website link

You can visit my website [here](https://jetbrains.com)

#adding image

![This is an image](images/clipboard-1934544978.jpeg){width="449"}

This is an R Markdown document. *Markdown*(itilas) is a simple **formatting**(bold) syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

```{r}
2+2
```

#shotcut for code chek is ctrl+alt i

```{r}

```

#how to write inline code

```{r}
max(women$height)
```

The maximum women height is r `max(women$height)`

# how to input Tables or print table

```{r}




flextable::flextable(women)
gt::gt(women)
reactable::reactable(women)



```

#making plots

```{r}
gglot(women) + geom_point(aes(height, weight))
```


#introduction to piple

```{r}
pacman::p_load(tidyverse,
               gt,
               outbreaks)
```



#Earthquakes dataset
quakes

#Task:show the top 5 earthquakes (magnitude)

#OPT 1: clear, but long


```{r}

arranged_data <- arrange(quakes, -mag)
head(arrange(quakes, -mag), n = 5)
```


arranged_data <- arrange(quakes, -ma


```{r}
women

select(height)
```
